schedule.description: Cubs slide analysis — dropped from first, 4 straight losses
Boyd meniscus surgery — rotation in crisis
Game 3 preview — Cabrera vs Harrison, sweep on the line
Peralta trade urgency — 12 days to June 1
Game time hype — series finale at Wrigley

stories.title: Brewers 5, Cubs 2 — Misiorowski Dominant Again, Cubs' Slide Hits Four
stories.angle: Misiorowski extended his scoreless streak to 24.1 innings with 6 shutout frames (8 Ks, 3 hits). Ben Brown (L, 1-2) allowed 3 runs in 5 innings including his league-leading 6th wild pitch. Turang 3-for-4 with a 2-run HR. Cubs 29-20, Brewers 28-18 (1st in NL Central). Four straight losses, eight of last ten.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Brewers 5, Cubs 2.

Misiorowski: 6 IP, 0 ER, 8 Ks, 3 hits. That's 24.1 consecutive scoreless innings. Just unhittable.

Ben Brown took the loss — 5 IP, 3 ER, 7 hits, his league-leading 6th wild pitch. Cubs have lost four straight.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Brewers 5, Cubs 2 — Misiorowski Dominant Again
**Key facts + sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|
| Final score: Brewers 5, Cubs 2 | MLB.com Gameday, CBS Sports, ESPN | HIGH |
| Misiorowski: 6 IP, 0 ER, 8 Ks, 3 singles | CBS Chicago recap, WTMJ | HIGH |
| Misiorowski scoreless streak: 24.1 consecutive innings | WTMJ headline ("24 1/3 innings") | HIGH |
| Misiorowski record: 4-2 (W) | CBS Chicago, first search result | HIGH |
| Brice Turang: 3-for-4, 2-run HR | CBS Chicago | HIGH |
| Jake Bauers: 2 hits, RBI | CBS Chicago | HIGH |
| Garrett Mitchell: 1 RBI | CBS Chicago | HIGH |
| Ben Brown: 5 IP, 3 ER, 7 H, 2 BB, 6 K, 82 pitches, L(1-2) | CBS Chicago | HIGH |
| Brown's wild pitch was his league-leading 6th of the season | CBS Chicago ("league-leading sixth wild pitch") | HIGH |
| Wild pitch scored Chourio from third, with Mitchell at bat | CBS Chicago | HIGH |
| Cubs: 4 straight losses, 8 of last 10 | CBS Chicago recap | HIGH |
| Brewers moved into first place (28-18) | CBS Chicago, series context JSON | HIGH |
| Cubs fall to 29-20 | Series context JSON, derived | HIGH |
| Brewers lead NL Central by 0.5 games | GB formula: (28-29 wins + 20-18 losses)/2 = 0.5 | CONFIRMED |
**Cross-reference note:** Score confirmed via both MLB.com Gameday ("Brewers 5, Cubs 2 Final Score 05/19/2026") and CBS Sports box score URL. Brown's wild-pitch stat is the highest-prominence claim from a single source; the "league-leading" qualifier comes only from CBS Chicago recap. I am using it but flagging for verification in fact-check log.

stories.title: Cubs Drop From First, Brewers Take the Lead — Slide Reaches Crisis Point
stories.angle: Bold take on the Cubs' freefall. From NL Central leaders to 0.5 GB in second place. Lost four straight. With a potential series sweep tonight, the window to reassert themselves is tonight at Wrigley.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Four straight losses. Eight in the last ten. The Brewers are in first place by half a game.

The Cubs dropped from first to half a game back. Now they're scrambling to avoid a sweep. Wrigley wants answers tonight.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: Matt Boyd Out Six Weeks — Meniscus Surgery, Rotation Depth Critical
stories.angle: Boyd tore his left meniscus playing with his kids, underwent surgery, out approximately six weeks (second IL stint of the season). With Steele past the All-Star break, Horton 15-16 months out, and now Boyd six weeks, the rotation is in crisis mode.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Matt Boyd is out six weeks after tearing his meniscus playing with his kids.

Second IL stint of the season. Every time this rotation looks stable, something breaks.

The case for a Freddy Peralta trade is writing itself.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Matt Boyd Meniscus Surgery
**Key facts + sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|
| Boyd tore left meniscus playing with his kids | Yahoo Sports, NBC News, MLB.com | HIGH |
| Boyd underwent successful surgery | MLB Trade Rumors, ESPN, MLB.com | HIGH |
| Boyd expected to miss ~6 weeks | MLB Trade Rumors, ESPN, Craig Counsell quote | HIGH |
| "Probably closer to six weeks" — Craig Counsell | MLB.com, ESPN | HIGH |
| This is Boyd's second IL stint this season | Multiple sources mention "second time" this season | HIGH |
| Boyd previously on IL earlier in 2026 | Research notes from May 17-19 pipeline | HIGH |
| Justin Steele: 60-day IL (UCL revision), return after All-Star break | FanGraphs Injury Report | HIGH |
| Cade Horton: TJ surgery April 16, 15-16 months | Multiple sources | HIGH |
| Hunter Harvey: 60-day IL (triceps stress reaction) | ESPN transactions, SportsMockery | HIGH |
**Note:** The May 19 pipeline had Boyd with a "bullpen session expected this week." The meniscus tear supersedes that — clearly a new development after that pipeline ran.

stories.title: Game 3 Tonight — Cabrera vs Harrison, Series Sweep on the Line
stories.angle: Series finale at Wrigley, 6:40 PM CT. Kyle Harrison (4-1, 2.09 ERA, ≤2 ER in all 8 starts) faces Edward Cabrera (3-1, 4.06 ERA, HR in 5 straight starts). Cubs must win to avoid being swept at home.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Edward Cabrera vs Kyle Harrison tonight at Wrigley — series finale, 6:40 PM CT.

Harrison hasn't allowed more than 2 runs in any of his 8 starts this year. Cabrera has given up a HR in five straight.

No. 1 on today's task list: avoid a sweep.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Game 3 Preview — Cabrera vs Harrison
**Key facts + sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|
| Game 3, series finale (Cubs vs Brewers, May 18-20) | Series context JSON, cubsinsider.com | HIGH |
| Wrigley Field, 6:40 PM CT | Series context JSON | HIGH |
| Kyle Harrison starts for Brewers | DraftKings probable pitchers May 20, cubsinsider | HIGH |
| Harrison: 4-1, 2.09 ERA, 1.19 WHIP | DraftKings, Brew Crew Ball | HIGH |
| Harrison: 8 starts, ≤2 ER in every start | Brew Crew Ball ("has yet to allow more than two earned runs this season, spanning eight starts and 38.2 innings") | HIGH |
| Edward Cabrera starts for Cubs | DraftKings, cubsinsider | HIGH |
| Cabrera: 3-1, 4.06 ERA, 1.31 WHIP | cubsinsider series preview, DraftKings | HIGH (see note) |
| Cabrera: HR in 5 straight starts | DraftKings preview | HIGH |
| Cubs 0-2 in series (lost Games 1 and 2) | Series context + May 19 recap | HIGH |
**Note on Cabrera record:** One search result projected "8-7, 3.53 ERA" which appears to be a full-season projection or hallucination. Series preview sources (cubsinsider, DraftKings) consistently show 3-1, 4.06 ERA for today. Not citing W-L in tweet; only citing HR-in-5-straight which has strong sourcing.
**Note on Harrison origin:** One source mentions Harrison "came over from the Red Sox in an offseason trade." This is from a single search result summary and was NOT included in any tweet text. Requires independent verification before citing.

stories.title: Freddy Peralta Trade Clock — 12 Days to Mets' June 1 Deadline
stories.angle: Boyd's meniscus surgery sharpens the urgency. With Horton out 16 months, Steele past the All-Star break, and now Boyd 6 weeks, the rotation is unsustainable without a frontline arm. Peralta (30, pending FA, Counsell connection) is the dream target and the Mets' decision window closes June 1.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Freddy Peralta is the dream target. The Mets' June 1 deadline is 12 days away.

Horton out 16 months. Steele past the break. Boyd out six weeks. The rotation can't survive without a No. 1. Peralta fits, and Counsell knows him cold.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

| Boyd's meniscus as new urgency hook | New development (day of) | HIGH |

stories.title: Game Time — Series Finale, Avoid the Sweep
stories.angle: Pre-game urgency post. Cubs down 0-2 in series, half game behind in division. Cabrera must be his best. 6:40 PM CT.

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Game 3. Wrigley. 6:40 PM CT.

The Cubs need Edward Cabrera at his best — not the guy who's given up a HR in five straight starts.

Down 0-2 in the series, a half-game behind in the division. This one matters.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
